query: What is the capacity threshold that triggers the large generation interconnection process in NYISO according to the NYISO process manual 23 and guide UG21? Cite the relevant source report and section you got this from
final_answer:
Here is the answer, based on the relevant documents:

## NYISO Large Generation Interconnection Capacity Threshold

The capacity threshold that triggers the **large generation interconnection process** in NYISO is **20 MW**.

### Under the Legacy Framework (Manual 23 / Attachment X – LFIP)

The **Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)** set forth in **Attachment X** of the NYISO OATT apply to generating facilities that **exceed 20 MW**. This is stated directly in the title and scope of Attachment X:

> **"30 Attachment X – Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Class Year Transmission Facilities)"**
> — *NYISO OATT, Section 30 Attachment X* (Source: https://nyisoviewer.etariff.biz/viewerdoclibrary/temp/71961cdc-6da5-445e-b75b-c2ae8294e04c.pdf)

Additionally, **Section 30.3.1** of Attachment X specifies that an Interconnection Request must be submitted for:

> *"(1) material modifications; **(2) increases in capacity that results in total output in excess of 20 MW;** and (3) Transmission Projects initially evaluated pursuant to Attachment P..."*
> — *NYISO OATT, Section 30.3.1* (Source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%20Section%2030_HSR%20Revisions.pdf)

Conversely, facilities **20 MW or smaller** were processed under the **Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP)** in Attachment Z. Under Attachment Z, facilities larger than 2 MW but no larger than 20 MW go through the "Study Process," while facilities ≤2 MW may use the "Fast Track Process."

### Under Guide UG-21 (Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User's Guide)

The UG-21 Guide (Version 3.0, effective July 18, 2025) similarly notes that attestations are required **"for Generating Facilities greater than 20 MW"** in reference to the NYSRC Reliability Rule B.5 requirements, confirming the 20 MW demarcation point.

Source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf, Section 6

### Important Note on Recent Changes

Effective **May 2, 2024**, the NYISO filed new **Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP)** under new **OATT Attachment HH** (FERC Docket No. ER24-1915) in response to FERC Order No. 2023. Under Attachment HH, **all generating facilities** — regardless of size — now proceed through a unified **Cluster Study Process**, eliminating the separate LFIP/SGIP tracks. However, as noted in Manual 23 (v4.3, February 2025), the legacy procedures in Section 3 of the TEI Manual continue to apply for ongoing Class Year 2023 projects and Load projects during the transition period. Manual 23 is currently being revised to incorporate the new Attachment HH requirements.

### Summary

| Threshold | Process |
|---|---|
| **> 20 MW** | Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP) — OATT Attachment X |
| **≤ 20 MW** | Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP) — OATT Attachment Z |
| **2014–present** | Under new Attachment HH (SIP), all generating facilities unified into Cluster Study Process |

From the search results, I can see:

1. **NYISO OATT Attachment X, Section 30** - says "Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Merchant Transmission Facilities)" - This clearly establishes **20 MW** as the threshold for large generation interconnection.

2. **NYISO OATT Section 30.3.1** (from the May 20, 2024 ICAP/MIWG/PRLWG document) - states: "The requirement to submit an Interconnection Request applies to all Large Facilities seeking evaluation under this Attachment X to the ISO OATT, including (1) material modifications; (2) increases in capacity that results in total output in excess of 20 MW..."

NYISO "Large Facility" "exceed 20 MW" OR "greater than 20 MW" interconnection threshold

steps.reasoning:
Now I have all the information needed to answer the question. Let me synthesize the findings:

Under the legacy framework (NYISO Manual 23 / Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual and OATT Attachment X - Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures):

1. **Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)** apply to **Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs**. This is clear from:
   - OATT Attachment X, Section 30: "Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Class Year Transmission Facilities)"
   - OATT Section 30.3.1: The requirement to submit an Interconnection Request applies to "increases in capacity that results in total output in excess of 20 MW"

2. **Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP)** (OATT Attachment Z) apply to:
   - Facilities no larger than 2 MW → Fast Track Process
   - Facilities larger than 2 MW but no larger than 20 MW → Study Process
   - So facilities up to 20 MW are considered "Small Generating Facilities"

So the threshold is **20 MW** - generating facilities exceeding 20 MW trigger the Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP).

Under the **new Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP)** in Attachment HH (effective May 2, 2024, replacing Attachments X and Z), all generating facilities - regardless of size - now proceed through the same Cluster Study Process. However, the question specifically asks about "the large generation interconnection process" according to Manual 23 and Guide UG21, which refer to the LFIP framework.

Let me cite the specific sources:

**Source 1: NYISO OATT Section 30 (Attachment X)** - The header states: "30 Attachment X – Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Class Year Transmission Facilities)"

**Source 2: NYISO OATT Section 30.3.1** - States that Interconnection Request requirement applies to "increases in capacity that results in total output in excess of 20 MW"

**Source 3: UG-21 Guide** - References that attestations are required "for Generating Facilities greater than 20 MW"
